title: Maya G. Torres — Post-specialist follow-up (visit 5)
description: Fifth visit, first PCP check-in after pediatric neurology: gabapentin 100 mg added plus multimodal plan (chiropractic, CBT, physical therapy). PedMIDAS 34 → 18 and frequency falling — improving.

history.label: Prior PCP headache visits (summary)
history.text: Four PCP headache visits since February for chronic headaches. PedMIDAS 44 (02/05) → 40 (03/12) → 38 (04/30) → 34 (06/05). Referral to pediatric neurology placed 06/05 for persistent disability despite lifestyle plan and riboflavin.

history.label: Pediatric neurology consult — Dr. Lin, Children's Neuro Clinic
history.text: Chronic migraine without aura confirmed. Gabapentin 100 mg nightly started. Advised chiropractic care, CBT with psychology, and physical therapy. Neurology follow-up in 3 months (approx 2026-09-20).

history.label: Chiropractic progress note
history.text: Attending weekly since 06/24. Cervical tension reduced; patient reports fewer headache days. Tolerating care well.

history.label: Physical therapy note
history.text: Started 06/27, attending twice weekly. Posture and neck-strengthening program; home exercises done most days.

history.label: Psychology / CBT note
history.text: CBT started 07/01. Learning relaxation and coping strategies; using them at school. Mood improved.

history.label: Vitals (today)
history.text: BP 100/62, HR 76, T 36.8C, Ht 145 cm, Wt 38 kg. Appears well.

red_flag_catalog.label: Sudden severe (thunderclap) / worst headache of life
Wakes from sleep / worst on awakening
Vision changes (double vision, non-aura vision loss)
Fever, stiff neck, or systemic infection signs
History of cancer, immune suppression, or shunt

pedmidas_questions.question: Full school days missed in last 3 months
Partial school days missed in last 3 months
School days functioning at less than half
Days unable to do home activities/chores
Days participated in activities at less than half

evidence.quote: Gabapentin 100 mg nightly started. Advised chiropractic care, CBT with psychology, and physical therapy.
Attending weekly since 06/24. Cervical tension reduced; patient reports fewer headache days.
Started 06/27, attending twice weekly. Posture and neck-strengthening program; home exercises done most days.
CBT started 07/01. Learning relaxation and coping strategies; using them at school. Mood improved.
She says much better — only about four headache days in the last month.
Taking gabapentin every night; a little sleepy the first week, nothing since.
No night waking, no vomiting, no vision changes — nothing scary.
One partial school day missed since June; back in PE and sleeping nine hours.
We kept the diary; ibuprofen only twice this month.

Cervical tension reduced; patient reports fewer headache days.
Posture and neck-strengthening program; home exercises done most days.

brief_lines.text: Seen by pediatric neurology (Dr. Lin, Children's Neuro Clinic) on 06/20 — chronic migraine without aura confirmed.
Gabapentin 100 mg nightly added by specialist 06/20.
Multimodal therapy underway per specialist plan: chiropractic (since 06/24), PT (since 06/27), CBT (since 07/01) — progress notes favorable.
PedMIDAS improving across 4 prior PCP visits: 44 → 40 → 38 → 34; re-score today expected lower.
Specialist follow-up in 3 months (~09/20); PCP 1-month progress check suggested for mid-August.

summary: Visit 5, first PCP check-in after pediatric neurology: PedMIDAS 34 → 18 since specialist plan started 06/20. [evb-1, evb-6, evb-9]
Gabapentin 100 mg nightly tolerated — mild first-week sleepiness resolved; family adherent. [evb-2, evb-7]
Chiropractic, physical therapy, and CBT all underway per specialist plan with favorable progress notes. [evb-3, evb-4, evb-5]
Headache frequency ~4 days/month; ibuprofen ~2 days/month — no overuse concern. [evb-6, evb-10]
Lifestyle improved (regular sleep, hydration, PE resumed); red-flag screen negative today. [evb-8, evb-9]

Continue chiropractic, physical therapy, and CBT as advised by neurology

referral_considerations: Already specialist-connected — neurology follow-up confirmed ~09/20 (clinician to confirm cadence)
Contact neurology sooner if trend reverses, red flags appear, or gabapentin side effects emerge
patient_instructions: The plan is working — keep going
Give gabapentin every night exactly as Dr. Lin prescribed
Keep the chiropractic, physical therapy, and CBT appointments and home exercises
Keep the headache diary going until the next visit
Return sooner for night-waking headaches, morning vomiting, or sudden changes
care_plan.disclaimer: Draft for clinician review. Final clinical decisions remain with the clinician.

canned_qa.answer: Since the June 5 pre-specialist visit: neurology started gabapentin 100 mg nightly and a multimodal plan (chiropractic, CBT, physical therapy). PedMIDAS improved 34 → 18, headache frequency is down to ~4 days/month, lifestyle has improved (regular sleep, hydration, PE resumed), and the red-flag screen remains negative.

At the 06/20 consult, Dr. Lin (Children's Neuro Clinic) confirmed chronic migraine without aura, started gabapentin 100 mg nightly, and advised chiropractic care, CBT with psychology, and physical therapy, with neurology follow-up in 3 months. All three therapies are underway with favorable progress notes.

Two follow-ups are on the plan: the neurology follow-up with Dr. Lin is confirmed for about 3 months after the consult (~2026-09-20), and the draft PCP plan suggests a 1-month check-in (mid-August) to review progress and the overall well-being of the child — clinician to confirm.

Gabapentin 100 mg nightly was started by neurology on 06/20 and is specialist-managed. The family reports she takes it every night; she was a little sleepy in the first week but has had no side effects since. Any dose changes should go through Dr. Lin.

insights.note: Established migraine, clearly improving on specialist-led multimodal plan — protect continuity. Clinician review required.
